name: HBV_epsilon_stemloop
description: HBV epsilon (ε) encapsidation signal: a conserved stem-loop structure at nt 1849-1907 of the pgRNA (located on the DNA template within ORF C). Contains a basal stem, internal bulge, apical stem and apical loop. Required for pgRNA encapsidation into the nucleocapsid and serves as the template for priming of (−)-strand DNA synthesis: the polymerase covalently links the first dNTP of the (−)-strand to a tyrosine residue using the bulge sequence as template. Mutations in the basal stem destroy replication. Sequence-level conservation across all 10 HBV genotypes.
expected_copy_count: 1 ε per genome, also present in the terminal redundancy of pgRNA
